Lion's Paw: The massive lion paws carved into the rock, marking the entrance to the final climb to the summit.
Mirror Wall: A highly polished white masonry wall that was once so shiny that the king could see his reflection in it.
Sigiriya Frescoes: Exquisite 5th-century paintings of celestial maidens, world-famous for their grace and vibrant colors.
Summit (Sky Palace): The top of the rock containing the ruins of King Kassapa's palace, offering 360-degree views of the jungle.
Water Gardens: Symmetrical gardens with ancient hydraulic systems, fountains, and pools that still work today during rain.
Boulder Gardens: Natural rock formations integrated into the landscape, once used as Buddhist monasteries before the palace was built.
Terrace Gardens: Series of cultivated terraces that transition from the level water gardens to the rugged boulder gardens.
Cobra Hood Cave: A natural rock shelter with a shape resembling a cobra's hood, featuring ancient paintings and inscriptions.
Audience Hall: A large flat rock surface where the king held meetings, featuring a throne carved directly out of the stone.
Pidurangala Rock: A sister rock offering the best panoramic view of Sigiriya, home to an ancient Buddhist forest monastery.
Sigiriya Museum: A modern museum showcasing artifacts, models of the site, and the historical context of the Sigiriya kingdom.
